items.title: SquareMind raises $18M for robotic dermatology platform
items.summary: Squaremind has raised funding to launch Swan, a robotic platform that automates full-body skin imaging for early cancer detection. The post SquareMind raises $18M for robotic dermatology platform appeared first on The Robot Report .

items.title: Learn about the latest advances in physical AI at the Robotics Summit
items.summary: The Robotics Summit & Expo will have an entire track of sessions focusing on how to apply artificial intelligence to robotics. The post Learn about the latest advances in physical AI at the Robotics Summit appeared first on The Robot Report .

items.title: CAbLECAR: efficiently scheduling QLDPC codes on a tileable spin qubit chip with shuttling
items.summary: Semiconductor spin qubits are a promising platform for large-scale quantum computing, but have yet to take full advantage of the broad class of quantum low-density parity check (QLDPC) codes, which promise high encoding rates and efficient logic but require nonlocal connectivity between physical qubits.

items.title: SpecRLBench: A Benchmark for Generalization in Specification-Guided Reinforcement Learning
items.summary: Specification-guided reinforcement learning (RL) provides a principled framework for encoding complex, temporally extended tasks using formal specifications such as linear temporal logic (LTL). While recent methods have shown promising results, their ability to generalize across unseen specifications and diverse environments remains insufficiently understood.

items.title: Passage-Aware Structural Mapping for RGB-D Visual SLAM
items.summary: Doorways and passages are critical structural elements for indoor robot navigation, yet they remain underexplored in modern Visual SLAM (VSLAM) frameworks. This paper presents a passage-aware structural mapping approach for RGB-D VSLAM that detects doors and traversable openings by jointly fusing geometric, semantic, and topological cues.

items.title: Exploiting Differential Flatness for Efficient Learning-based Model Predictive Control of Constrained Multi-Input Control Affine Systems
items.summary: Learning-based control techniques use data from past trajectories to control systems with uncertain dynamics. However, learning-based controllers are often computationally inefficient, limiting their practicality. To address this limitation, we propose a learning-based controller that exploits differential flatness, a property of many robotic systems.

items.title: Sereact gets Series B funding to expand Cortex 2.0 robot brain, enter U.S. market
items.summary: Sereact plans to expand Cortex 2.0 from bin picking to tasks such as assembly and kitting, and it is opening a Boston office. The post Sereact gets Series B funding to expand Cortex 2.0 robot brain, enter U.S. market appeared first on The Robot Report .

items.title: Learning Human-Intention Priors from Large-Scale Human Demonstrations for Robotic Manipulation
items.summary: Human videos contain rich manipulation priors, but using them for robot learning remains difficult because raw observations entangle scene understanding, human motion, and embodiment-specific action. We introduce MoT-HRA, a hierarchical vision-language-action framework that learns human-intention priors from large-scale human demonstrations.

items.title: Pushing Radar Odometry Beyond the Pavement: Current Capabilities and Challenges
items.summary: Radar offers unique advantages for localization in unstructured environments, including robustness to weather, lighting, and airborne particulates. While most prior work has studied radar odometry in urban, largely planar settings, its performance in off-road environments remains less understood.

items.title: Agent-Centric Visual Reinforcement Learning under Dynamic Perturbations
items.summary: Visual reinforcement learning aims to empower an agent to learn policies from visual observations, yet it remains vulnerable to dynamic visual perturbations, such as unpredictable shifts in corruption types.

items.title: Computational Design and Co-Robotic Fabrication for Material Reuse in Architecture
items.summary: Climate change and resource depletion demand a shift from the dominant linear "take-make-use-dispose" paradigm of construction toward circular, low-waste practices. Material reuse offers a promising pathway by reducing raw material extraction, mitigating waste, and extending the service lifespan of carbon-sequestering materials such as timber.

items.title: Real-time windrow detection from onboard tractor sensors for automated following
items.summary: Proprietary design in commercial windrow-detection systems restricts transparency and limits progress in open autonomous forage-harvesting research. We present a multi-modal dataset combining stereo vision and LiDAR from tractor-mounted sensors during real baling operations.

items.title: CF-VLA: Efficient Coarse-to-Fine Action Generation for Vision-Language-Action Policies
items.summary: Flow-based vision-language-action (VLA) policies offer strong expressivity for action generation, but suffer from a fundamental inefficiency: multi-step inference is required to recover action structure from uninformative Gaussian noise, leading to a poor efficiency-quality trade-off under real-time constraints. We address this issue by rethinking the role of the starting point in generative action modeling.

items.title: Hybrid A*-Based Reverse Path-Planning of a Vehicle with Trailer System
items.summary: Reverse parking maneuvering of a vehicle with trailer system is a difficult task to complete for human drivers due to the multi-body nature of the system and the unintuitive controls required to orientate the trailer properly. The problem is complicated with the presence of other vehicles that the trailer and its connected vehicle must avoid during the reverse parking maneuver.

items.title: Towards Lawful Autonomous Driving: Deriving Scenario-Aware Driving Requirements from Traffic Laws and Regulations
items.summary: Driving in compliance with traffic laws and regulations is a basic requirement for human drivers, yet autonomous vehicles (AVs) can violate these requirements in diverse real-world scenarios. To encode law compliance into AV systems, conventional approaches use formal logic languages to explicitly specify behavioral constraints, but this process is labor-intensive, hard to scale, and costly to maintain.

items.title: Interoceptive machine framework: Toward interoception-inspired regulatory architectures in artificial intelligence
items.summary: This review proposes an integrative framework grounded on interoception and embodied AI-termed the interoceptive machine framework-that translates biologically inspired principles of internal-state regulation into computational architectures for adaptive autonomy.

items.title: Sliding Mode Control for Safe Trajectory Tracking with Moving Obstacles Avoidance: Experimental Validation on Planar Robots
items.summary: This paper presents a unified control framework for robust trajectory tracking and moving obstacle avoidance applicable to a broad class of mobile robots. By formulating a generalized kinematic transformation, we convert diverse vehicle dynamics into a strict feedback form, facilitating the design of a Sliding Mode Control (SMC) strategy for precise and robust reference tracking.

items.title: Guiding Vector Field Generation via Score-based Diffusion Model
items.summary: Guiding Vector Fields (GVFs) are a powerful tool for robotic path following. However, classical methods assume smooth, ordered curves and fail when paths are unordered, multi-branch, or generated by probabilistic models. We propose a unified framework, termed the Score-Induced Guiding Vector Field (SGVF), which leverages score-based generative modeling to construct vector fields directly from data distributions.

items.title: Scheduling and Routing in the Flexible Job Shop with Heterogeneous Transbots and Zoning: A Constraint Programming Approach
items.summary: Coordinating production and material transfers is increasingly important in modern manufacturing systems equipped with mobile transfer robots, known as transbots. This study considers a flexible job shop environment in which heterogeneous transbots transport parts between machines.

items.title: SPLIT: Separating Physical-Contact via Latent Arithmetic in Image-Based Tactile Sensors
items.summary: Training machine learning models for robotic tactile sensing requires vast amounts of data, yet obtaining realistic interaction data remains a challenge due to physical complexity and variability. Simulating tactile sensors is thus a crucial step in accelerating progress. This paper presents SPLIT, a novel method for simulating image-based tactile sensors, with a primary focus on the DIGIT sensor.

items.title: Characterizing Vision-Language-Action Models across XPUs: Constraints and Acceleration for On-Robot Deployment
items.summary: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models are promising for generalist robot control, but on-robot deployment is bottlenecked by real-time inference under tight cost and energy budgets. Most prior evaluations rely on desktop-grade GPUs, obscuring the trade-offs and opportunities offered by heterogeneous edge accelerators (GPUs/XPUs/NPUs).

items.title: An Automatic Ground Collision Avoidance System with Reinforcement Learning
items.summary: This article evaluates an artificial intelligence (AI)-based Automatic Ground Collision Avoidance System (AGCAS) designed for advanced jet trainers to enhance operational effectiveness. In the continuously evolving field of aerospace engineering, the integration of AI is crucial for advancing operations with improved timing constraints and efficiency.

items.title: FreqCache: Accelerating Embodied VLN Models with Adaptive Frequency-Guided Token Caching
items.summary: Vision-Language-Navigation (VLN) models exhibit excellent navigation accuracy but incur high computational overhead. Token caching has emerged as a promising training-free strategy to reduce this cost by reusing token computation results; however, existing token caching approaches rely on visual domain methods for cacheable token selection, leading to challenges when adapted to VLN models.

items.title: Pedestrians play chicken with an autonomous vehicle
items.summary: Automated vehicles (AVs) are commonly programmed to yield unconditionally to pedestrians in the interest of safety. However, this design choice can give rise to the Freezing Robot Problem in which pedestrians learn to assert priority at every interaction, causing vehicles to stall and make no progress.

items.title: ARETE: Attention-based Rasterized Encoding for Topology Estimation using HSV-transformed Crowdsourced Vehicle Fleet Data
items.summary: The continuous advancement of autonomous driving (AD) introduces challenges across multiple disciplines to ensure safe and efficient driving. One such challenge is the generation of High-Definition (HD) maps, which must remain up to date and highly accurate for downstream automotive tasks. One promising approach is the use of crowdsourced data from a vehicle fleet, representing road topology and lane-level features.

items.title: Look back on 10 years of legged robots with Ghost Robotics at the Robotics Summit
items.summary: At the summit, Gavin Kenneally, CEO and co-founder of Ghost Robotics, will share lessons from 10 years of deploying quadruped robots. The post Look back on 10 years of legged robots with Ghost Robotics at the Robotics Summit appeared first on The Robot Report .

items.title: ABB Robotics launches PoWa cobot family targeting industrial tasks
items.summary: ABB Robotics said its new PoWa family of cobots addresses a long‑standing gap in the market between traditional cobots. The post ABB Robotics launches PoWa cobot family targeting industrial tasks appeared first on The Robot Report .

items.title: From sci-fi to reality: Physical AI’s future with Dr. Jan Liphardt
items.summary: Dr. Jan Liphardt discusses the impact of modular AI systems on robotics, emphasizing the importance of transparency and safety in human-robot interactions. The post From sci-fi to reality: Physical AI’s future with Dr. Jan Liphardt appeared first on The Robot Report .

items.title: Accenture, Vodafone, and SAP to pilot humanoid robots in the warehouse
items.summary: The humanoids in the pilot are powered by Accenture’s Robot Brain solution, enabling them to interact naturally with human operators. The post Accenture, Vodafone, and SAP to pilot humanoid robots in the warehouse appeared first on The Robot Report .

items.title: SS Innovations is developing a drone-based surgical robot
items.summary: SS Innovations designed the SSi Vimana Aero to bring robotic surgical care directly to wounded soldiers. It is also working on a surgical humanoid. The post SS Innovations is developing a drone-based surgical robot appeared first on The Robot Report .

items.title: A Co-Evolutionary Theory of Human-AI Coexistence: Mutualism, Governance, and Dynamics in Complex Societies
items.summary: Classical robot ethics is often framed around obedience, most famously through Asimov's laws. This framing is too narrow for contemporary AI systems, which are adaptive, generative, embodied, and embedded in physical, psychological, and social worlds.

items.title: GM’s Mikell Taylor to lead Women in Robotics Breakfast at the Robotics Summit
items.summary: Mikell Taylor has spent 25 years making robots, from a robotic prom date to uncrewed underwater vehicles and industrial robotic systems. The post GM’s Mikell Taylor to lead Women in Robotics Breakfast at the Robotics Summit appeared first on The Robot Report .

items.title: Pudu Robotics raises nearly $150M as it targets industrial applications
items.summary: Pudu plans to use the funding to develop its embodied AI, grow its product portfolio, and expand in global markets beyond service robots. The post Pudu Robotics raises nearly $150M as it targets industrial applications appeared first on The Robot Report .

items.title: End of an era: Honeywell hands warehouse automation reins to AIP
items.summary: Honeywell to sell its Intelligrated and Transnorm warehouse automation brands to American Industrial Partners by late 2026. The post End of an era: Honeywell hands warehouse automation reins to AIP appeared first on The Robot Report .

items.title: Reliable Robotics raises funding for fully automated aircraft
items.summary: Reliable Robotics has raised $160 million as it continues to develop and work toward FAA certification for its Reliable Autonomy System for civilian and military aircraft. The post Reliable Robotics raises funding for fully automated aircraft appeared first on The Robot Report .

items.title: From EVs to robotics: Tesla targets 10M Optimus units with new Texas plant
items.summary: Tesla begins Optimus production in Q2 2026, replacing Fremont’s legacy car lines, breaking ground in Texas as it scales toward a robotics-first future. The post From EVs to robotics: Tesla targets 10M Optimus units with new Texas plant appeared first on The Robot Report .

items.title: Cruz’ing through the terminal: A&K Robotics lands $8M for autonomous mobility
items.summary: Investment is led by Business Development Bank of Canada’s Industrial Innovation Venture Fund and Vantage Futures to expand production and R&D capacity. The post Cruz’ing through the terminal: A&K Robotics lands $8M for autonomous mobility appeared first on The Robot Report .

items.title: Glydways brings in $170M to scale its AV technology
items.summary: Glydways plans to build networks of autonomous vehicle expressways that move people at 10 times the throughput of today's technology. The post Glydways brings in $170M to scale its AV technology appeared first on The Robot Report .

items.title: NVIDIA and Partners Showcase the Future of AI-Driven Manufacturing at Hannover Messe 2026
items.summary: Manufacturing is at an inflection point. Across every major industrial economy, the pressure to do more with less — due to faster design cycles, leaner operations and strain on skilled labor pools — is accelerating the shift to AI-driven production. The question is no longer whether to adopt AI, but how fast and at what […]

items.title: Learning Versatile Humanoid Manipulation with Touch Dreaming
items.summary: Humanoid robots promise general-purpose assistance, yet real-world humanoid loco-manipulation remains challenging because it requires whole-body stability, end-effector dexterity, and contact-aware interaction under frequent contact changes. In this work, we study dexterous, contact-rich humanoid loco-manipulation.

items.title: Complementarity by Construction: A Lie-Group Approach to Solving Quadratic Programs with Linear Complementarity Constraints
items.summary: Many problems in robotics require reasoning over a mix of continuous dynamics and discrete events, such as making and breaking contact in manipulation and locomotion. These problems are locally well modeled by linear complementarity quadratic programs (LCQPs), an extension to QPs that introduce complementarity constraints.

items.title: National Robotics Week — Latest Physical AI Research, Breakthroughs and Resources
items.summary: This National Robotics Week, NVIDIA is highlighting the breakthroughs that are bringing AI into the physical world — as well as the growing wave of robots transforming industries, from agricultural and manufacturing to energy and beyond. Advancements in robot learning, simulation and foundation models are accelerating development, enabling robots to move from training in virtual […]

items.title: Genie Sim PanoRecon: Fast Immersive Scene Generation from Single-View Panorama
items.summary: We present Genie Sim PanoRecon, a feed-forward Gaussian-splatting pipeline that delivers high-fidelity, low-cost 3D scenes for robotic manipulation simulation. The panorama input is decomposed into six non-overlapping cube-map faces, processed in parallel, and seamlessly reassembled.

items.title: Into the Omniverse: NVIDIA GTC Showcases Virtual Worlds Powering the Physical AI Era
items.summary: Editor’s note: This post is part of Into the Omniverse, a series focused on how developers, 3D practitioners, and enterprises can transform their workflows using the latest advances in OpenUSD and NVIDIA Omniverse. NVIDIA GTC last week showcased a turning point in physical AI: Robots, vehicles and factories are scaling from single use cases and […]

items.title: RoboECC: Multi-Factor-Aware Edge-Cloud Collaborative Deployment for VLA Models
items.summary: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models are mainstream in embodied intelligence but face high inference costs. Edge-Cloud Collaborative (ECC) deployment offers an effective fix by easing edge-device computing pressure to meet real-time needs.

items.title: NVIDIA GTC 2026: Live Updates on What’s Next in AI
items.summary: Rolling coverage from San Jose, including NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang’s keynote, news highlights, live demos and on‑the‑ground color through March 19.

items.title: From Simulation to Production: How to Build Robots With AI
items.summary: The latest open models and frameworks from NVIDIA bring together simulation, robot learning and embedded compute to accelerate cloud-to-robot workflows.

items.title: HeiSD: Hybrid Speculative Decoding for Embodied Vision-Language-Action Models with Kinematic Awareness
items.summary: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) Models have become the mainstream solution for robot control, but suffer from slow inference speeds. Speculative Decoding (SD) is a promising acceleration method which can be divided into two categories: drafter-based SD and retrieval-based SD.

items.title: Gecko Robotics lands the largest US Navy robotics deal yet
items.summary: Gecko Robotics inked a five-year deal to help the U.S. Navy monitor and predict needed maintenance on its fleet of ships.

items.title: As Open Models Spark AI Boom, NVIDIA Jetson Brings It to Life at the Edge
items.summary: The Cat 306 CR mini-excavator weighs just under eight tons and fits inside a standard shipping container. It’s the machine a contractor rents when the job site is tight: a utility trench near a foundation, a basement dig in a dense neighborhood. The cab is roughly the size of a phone booth. The operator sits […]

items.title: Qualcomm’s partnership with Neura Robotics is just the beginning
items.summary: Neura Robotics is going to build new robots on top of Qualcomm's new IQ10 processors that were released at CES.

items.title: ABB Robotics Taps NVIDIA Omniverse to Deliver Industrial‑Grade Physical AI at Scale
items.summary: ABB Robotics and NVIDIA today announced a breakthrough partnership that brings industrial‑grade physical AI to the factory floor. By integrating NVIDIA Omniverse libraries directly into its RobotStudio programming and simulation suite, ABB Robotics will now deliver physically accurate simulation capabilities in its platform, dramatically cutting engineering time, reducing deployment costs by up to 40% and […]

items.title: KERV: Kinematic-Rectified Speculative Decoding for Embodied VLA Models
items.summary: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models build a token-domain robot control paradigm, yet suffer from low speed. Speculative Decoding (SD) is an optimization strategy that can boost inference speed.

items.title: InCoM: Intent-Driven Perception and Structured Coordination for Mobile Manipulation
items.summary: Mobile manipulation is a fundamental capability for general-purpose robotic agents, requiring both coordinated control of the mobile base and manipulator and robust perception under dynamically changing viewpoints.

items.title: The Swarm Intelligence Freeway-Urban Trajectories (SWIFTraj) Dataset -- Part II: A Graph-Based Approach for Trajectory Connection
items.summary: In Part I of this companion paper series, we introduced SWIFTraj, a new open-source vehicle trajectory dataset collected using a unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) swarm. The dataset has two distinctive features. First, by connecting trajectories across consecutive UAV videos, it provides long-distance continuous trajectories, with the longest exceeding 4.5 km.

items.title: NVIDIA and Global Industrial Software Leaders Partner With India’s Largest Manufacturers to Drive AI Boom
items.summary: India is entering a new age of industrialization, as AI transforms how the world designs, builds and runs physical products and systems. The country is investing $134 billion dollars in new manufacturing capacity across construction, automotive, renewable energy and robotics, creating both a massive challenge and opportunity to build software-defined factories from day one. At […]

items.title: Upside Robotics is reducing fertilizer use and waste in corn crops
items.summary: Upside Robotics builds autonomous solar-powered robots that can help farmers reduce their fertilizer use by 70%.

items.title: Into the Omniverse: Physical AI Open Models and Frameworks Advance Robots and Autonomous Systems
items.summary: Open source has become essential for driving innovation in robotics and autonomy. By providing access to critical infrastructure — from simulation frameworks to AI models — NVIDIA is enabling collaborative development that accelerates the path to safer, more capable autonomous systems.

items.title: How YC-backed Bucket Robotics survived its first CES
items.summary: Now, the startup is turning its attention to building the business, fundraising, and striking commercial deals.

items.title: Oshen built the first ocean robot to collect data in a Category 5 hurricane
items.summary: Oshen has signed contracts with multiple government agencies for its C-Star robots to collect ocean data autonomously.

items.title: AI’s Next Revolution: Multiply Labs Is Scaling Robotics-Driven Cell Therapy Biomanufacturing Labs
items.summary: Startup works with leading cell therapy companies to bring robotics manufacturing into the clean room, reducing costs by more than 70% while accelerating output compared with legacy systems.

items.title: Inside CES 2026’s “physical AI” takeover
items.summary: After years of chatbots and image generators, AI is finally leaving the screen. At CES 2026, that shift became impossible to ignore.  The annual tech showcase in Las Vegas was dominated by “physical AI” and robotics, from Boston Dynamic’s newly redesigned Atlas humanoid robot to AI-powered ice makers (yes, really). The companies in attendance clearly want consumers […]

items.title: Japan Science and Technology Agency Develops NVIDIA-Powered Moonshot Robot for Elderly Care
items.summary: The next universal technology since the smartphone is on the horizon — and it may be a little less pocket friendly. The Moonshot research program, funded by the Japan Science and Technology Agency and accelerated by NVIDIA AI and robotics technologies, is working to create a world by 2050 where AI-powered, autonomously learning robots are […]

items.title: Steel, Sensors and Silicon: How Caterpillar Is Bringing Edge AI to the Jobsite
items.summary: At CES, Caterpillar reveals how it’s integrating NVIDIA technologies, from NVIDIA Jetson Thor to speech models, to transform the world’s heavy industries.

items.title: From Warehouse to Wallet: New State of AI in Retail and CPG Survey Uncovers How AI Is Rewiring Supply Chains and Customer Experiences
items.summary: AI has transformed retail and consumer packaged goods (CPG) operations, enhancing customer analysis and segmentation to enable greater personalization for marketing and advertising, and boosting the speed and accuracy of demand forecasting for supply chains and logistics. Companies are also raising the bar for customer engagement through intelligent digital shopping assistants and catalog enrichment by […]

items.title: NVIDIA Unveils New Open Models, Data and Tools to Advance AI Across Every Industry
items.summary: Expanding the open model universe, NVIDIA today released new open models, data and tools to advance AI across every industry. These models — spanning the NVIDIA Nemotron family for agentic AI, the NVIDIA Cosmos platform for physical AI, the new NVIDIA Alpamayo family for autonomous vehicle development, NVIDIA Isaac GR00T for robotics and NVIDIA Clara […]

items.title: Pickle Robot adds Tesla veteran as first CFO
items.summary: Jeff Evanson recently joined Pickle Robot as the company's first CFO as the startup has allegedly expanded its partnership with UPS.

items.title: Into the Omniverse: OpenUSD and NVIDIA Halos Accelerate Safety for Robotaxis, Physical AI Systems
items.summary: Physical AI is moving from research labs into the real world, powering intelligent robots and autonomous vehicles (AVs) — such as robotaxis — that must reliably sense, reason and act amid unpredictable conditions.

items.title: Cheers to AI: ADAM Robot Bartender Makes Drinks at Vegas Golden Knights Game
items.summary: In Las Vegas’s T-Mobile Arena, fans of the Golden Knights are getting more than just hockey — they’re getting a taste of the future. ADAM, a robot developed with NVIDIA Isaac libraries, is pouring drinks and turning heads in one of the NHL’s most exciting venues. ADAM, short for Automated Dual Arm Mixologist, was developed […]

items.title: 1X struck a deal to send its ‘home’ humanoids to factories and warehouses
items.summary: Despite launching as a humanoid robot designed to help consumers around the house, 1X's Neo robots are heading to industrial use cases.

items.title: Robots’ Holiday Wishes Come True: NVIDIA Jetson Platform Offers High-Performance Edge AI at Festive Prices
items.summary: Editor’s note: This blog has been updated to showcase additional recent innovations tapping into the NVIDIA Jetson platform. Developers, researchers, hobbyists and students can take a byte out of holiday shopping this season as NVIDIA has unwrapped special discounts on the NVIDIA Jetson family of developer kits for edge AI and robotics — available through […]

items.title: NVIDIA and AWS Expand Full-Stack Partnership, Providing the Secure, High-Performance Compute Platform Vital for Future Innovation
items.summary: At AWS re:Invent, NVIDIA and Amazon Web Services expanded their strategic collaboration with new technology integrations across interconnect technology, cloud infrastructure, open models and physical AI. As part of this expansion, AWS will support NVIDIA NVLink Fusion — a platform for custom AI infrastructure — for deploying its custom-designed silicon, including next-generation Trainium4 chips for […]

items.title: SPEAR-1: Scaling Beyond Robot Demonstrations via 3D Understanding
items.summary: Robotic Foundation Models (RFMs) hold great promise as generalist, end-to-end systems for robot control. Yet their ability to generalize across new environments, tasks, and embodiments remains limited. We argue that a major bottleneck lies in their foundations: most RFMs are built by fine-tuning internet-pretrained Vision-Language Models (VLMs).

items.title: This Khosla-backed startup can track drones, trucks, and robotaxis, inch by inch
items.summary: Point One Navigation, now valued at $230 million, is building out well beyond automotive.

items.title: Why a researcher is building robots that look and act like bats
items.summary: These palm-sized robots use ultrasound signals to navigate harsh conditions in search and rescue missions.

items.title: AI researchers ’embodied’ an LLM into a robot – and it started channeling Robin Williams
items.summary: AI researchers at Andon Labs embedded various LLMs in a vacuum robot to test how ready they were to be embodied. And hilarity ensued.

items.title: Coco Robotics taps UCLA professor to lead new physical AI research lab
items.summary: Coco Robotics is working toward automating its fleet of delivery robots using its millions of miles of collected data.

items.title: The world is just not quite ready for humanoids yet
items.summary: Despite the amount of money being injected into the industry, humanoids won't be able to learn dexterity -- or the fine motor movements with hands -- rendering them essentially useless.

items.title: SoftBank bulks up its robotics portfolio with ABB Group’s robotics unit
items.summary: SoftBank says this acquisition will help it dive deeper into robotics as it considers physical AI to be the next frontier.

items.title: Former OpenAI and DeepMind researchers raise whopping $300M seed to automate science
items.summary: Periodic Labs has raised from a tech industry who's who, including Andreessen Horowitz, Nvidia, Elad Gil, Jeff Dean, Eric Schmidt, and Jeff Bezos.

items.title: Live demo fails, AI safety wins, and the golden age of robotics
items.summary: This week on Equity, Anthony Ha, Kirsten Korosec, and Max Zeff unpack the biggest moves in AI, robotics, and regulation. Listen to the full episode to hear about: Equity will be back next week. Subscribe wherever you get your podcasts! Equity is TechCrunch’s flagship podcast, produced by Theresa Loconsolo, and posts every Wednesday and Friday. […]

items.title: Why Runway is eyeing the robotics industry for future revenue growth
items.summary: Runway is building up a robotics-focused team and fine-tuning its existing models for robotics and self-driving car customers.

items.title: Tesla is reportedly behind on its pledge to build 5,000 Optimus bots this year
items.summary: Tesla is behind on its goal to produce at least 5,000 Optimus humanoid robots by the end of 2025.

items.title: How TRIC Robotics is reducing pesticide use on strawberries using UV light
items.summary: TRIC Robotics just raised a $5.5 million seed round for its tractor-sized robots that help eliminate bacteria and pests on strawberry plants.

items.title: Diligent Robotics hires two notable Cruise alumni to its leadership team
items.summary: Diligent Robotics hired Cruise's former chief operating officer and former head of AI and robotics into c-suite positions.

items.title: More is less in unpercolated active solids
items.summary: A remarkable feat of active matter physics is that systems as diverse as collections of self-propelled particles, nematics mixed with molecular motors, and interacting robots can all be described by symmetry-based continuum theories. These descriptions rely on reducing complex effects of individual motors to a few key active parameters, which increase with activity.

items.title: Balancing Act: Trading Off Odometry and Map Registration for Efficient Lidar Localization
items.summary: Most autonomous vehicles rely on accurate and efficient localization, which is achieved by comparing live sensor data to a preexisting map, to navigate their environment. Balancing the accuracy of localization with computational efficiency remains a significant challenge, as high-accuracy methods often come with higher computational costs.

items.title: TwinLiteNet+: An Enhanced Multi-Task Segmentation Model for Autonomous Driving
items.summary: Semantic segmentation is a fundamental perception task in autonomous driving, particularly for identifying drivable areas and lane markings to enable safe navigation. However, most state-of-the-art (SOTA) models are computationally intensive and unsuitable for real-time deployment on resource-constrained embedded devices.
